sn46.52:3.4: I have not been able to identify either the hindrances or the awakening factors in any pre-Buddhist texts as sets, although several of the items are mentioned.
sn46.52:8.3: When the Buddha emphasizes his analytical method as a distinguishing point, it is not merely an intellectual exercise. Rather, a comprehensive analysis lays the groundwork for a comprehensive development, not neglecting any aspect. Pre-Buddhist texts such as the Upaniṣads lack such clear analytical treatment, relying on mystery and hidden truths to evoke understanding.
sn46.52:13.1: This refers to the first and second absorptions respectively.
sn46.52:15.1: This refers to the first absorption and all higher absortions respectively.
